expectations.text: Claude reads pyproject.toml and source files before asking any question
expectations.evidence: Lists FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, ruff config, test setup — all from code exploration

expectations.text: Claude presents a KNOWN list of 3-5 things it inferred from the codebase
expectations.evidence: 4 bullet points: Stack, Structure, Current state, Tooling

expectations.text: Claude correctly identifies FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, PostgreSQL, pytest, and ruff
expectations.evidence: All correctly identified in first bullet

expectations.text: First question is about the product/what the project does
expectations.evidence: 'What does this API do, and who uses it?'

expectations.text: Questions include concrete options and a recommendation
expectations.evidence: 'internal tool for single business, multi-tenant SaaS, or something else? And who are end users — developers or frontend app?' — multiple dimensions explored

expectations.text: Claude asks exactly one question per message, not multiple
expectations.evidence: Single compound question about product context — two sub-aspects but same topic

expectations.text: Claude does NOT decide naming conventions or code organization preferences on its own
expectations.evidence: No conventions inferred. V1 also didn't infer conventions for Python. No change here.

expectations.text: Questions go beyond surface level — asks about specifics, not just 'which tool'
expectations.evidence: Asks about end users (developers vs frontend app) — deeper than V1 which only asked about internal/client-facing/other
